titleEn: Western Growers Center for Innovation & Technology Selects Sami Robotics for First Sponsored Residency at Reservoir Farms
titleZh: 西部种植者创新技术中心选定Sami Robotics入驻Reservoir Farms，推动田间机器人实地验证
summaryZh: 西部种植者创新技术中心（WGCIT）选定农业机器人初创公司Sami Robotics，作为首个赞助入驻Reservoir Farms的企业。Sami Robotics开发的多功能机器人平台初期聚焦采摘作业，将在Reservoir Farms的萨利纳斯基地与种植者合作进行实地测试和优化。该入驻项目提供共享研发空间、商业种植田块及约翰迪尔等合作伙伴设备，旨在加速技术从原型到可商用产品的转化。此举反映了美国农业科技领域对机器人解决劳动力短缺问题的重视，以及通过产业协作推动技术落地的趋势。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了美国农业科技领域通过产业协作加速机器人技术落地的典型模式，对智慧畜牧行业探索机器人应用、解决劳动力短缺问题具有借鉴意义。
contentFull: By Western Growers Share The Western Growers Center for Innovation & Technology (WGCIT) selected Sami Robotics, a startup developing a multifunctional robotic platform capable of performing a variety of field tasks to receive the first WGCIT-sponsored residency slot at Reservoir Farms. The startup’s initial focus will be on harvesting. The residency will allow Sami to refine and test its technology alongside growers in real-world conditions at Reservoir’s Salinas location. “Harvesting has long been the most challenging segment of mechanization for growers,” said Walt Duflock, SVP, Innovation at Western Growers. “We are confident that Pascal and Éric and their team at Sami Robotics will provide solutions that work, and there is no better place for them to field test their technology than at Reservoir Farms.” “We are honored to be the first company selected for this meaningful sponsorship,” said Pascal Labrecque, CEO and Co-Founder of Sami Robotics. “This support from WGCIT is a strong endorsement of our team’s dedication and of the importance of our mission to help growers address the future of agricultural labor through robotics.” MORE BY WESTERN GROWERS Market Watch November 18, 2023 Climate Robotics Wins AgSharks Pitch Competition By Western Growers|November 18, 2023 “Building agricultural robots is one thing; proving they can deliver value in real field conditions is another,” said Éric Lapalme, CPO and Co-Founder of Sami Robotics. “Reservoir Farms offers an exceptional environment to test, refine and improve our platform alongside growers. The feedback we receive during this residency will be instrumental in making our technology more capable, reliable and impactful.” Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Sabanto Inc. and Verdant Robotics Announce Technical Integration of Autonomous Tractor Operation with SharpShooter Plant-Level Precision Application Reservoir Farms helps startups get to the first viable product in less time and for less capital by providing shared R&D space; shared commercially grown field acreage; shared equipment from partners like John Deere and real-world grower feedback on products they are building. In May, Western Growers announced they will provide Reservoir Farms $1.5 million in partnership funding over the next three years. “Western Growers helped us turn Reservoir Farms into more than just a test field — rather the Olympic Village of agtech,” said Danny Bernstein, CEO of Reservoir Farms. “Their commitment to sponsoring residencies signals to founders that if you’re serious about solving real problems, there’s an ecosystem here ready to back you — giving high potential teams the chance to train in real grower conditions, iterate quickly, and bring technologies to market that actually work for growers.” 0 1 5 Western Growers Center for Innovation & Technology Selects Sami Robotics for First Sponsored Residency at Reservoir Farms Subscribe Today For Subscribe Founded in 1926, Western Growers represents local and regional family farmers growing fresh produce in California, Arizona, Colorado and New Mexico. Western Growers' members and their workers provide over half the nation's fresh fruits, vegetables and tree nuts, including half of America's fresh organic produce. See all author stories here.
translationZh:
西部种植者创新技术中心（WGCIT）选定Sami Robotics——一家开发能够执行多种田间任务的多功能机器人平台的初创公司——获得Reservoir Farms首个WGCIT赞助入驻名额。该初创公司的初期重点将放在采摘作业上。此次入驻将使Sami能够在Reservoir位于萨利纳斯的地点，与种植者一起在真实世界条件下完善和测试其技术。

“采摘长期以来一直是种植者在机械化过程中最具挑战性的环节，”西部种植者创新高级副总裁Walt Duflock表示。“我们相信Pascal和Éric以及他们在Sami Robotics的团队将提供行之有效的解决方案，而Reservoir Farms是他们进行技术实地测试的最佳地点。”

“我们很荣幸成为首个入选这一有意义赞助项目的公司，”Sami Robotics首席执行官兼联合创始人Pascal Labrecque表示。“WGCIT的这份支持是对我们团队奉献精神的有力认可，也证明了我们帮助种植者通过机器人技术应对农业劳动力未来挑战这一使命的重要性。”

“制造农业机器人是一回事；证明它们能在真实田间条件下创造价值则是另一回事，”Sami Robotics首席产品官兼联合创始人Éric Lapalme表示。“Reservoir Farms提供了一个绝佳的环境，让我们能够与种植者一起测试、完善和改进我们的平台。在入驻期间收到的反馈将极大地帮助我们使技术更强大、更可靠、更具影响力。”

Reservoir Farms通过提供共享研发空间、共享商业种植田块、来自约翰迪尔等合作伙伴的共享设备以及种植者对正在开发产品的真实反馈，帮助初创公司以更少的时间和更低的成本实现首个可行产品。今年5月，西部种植者宣布将在未来三年内向Reservoir Farms提供150万美元的合作资金。

“西部种植者帮助我们使Reservoir Farms不仅仅是一个试验田——而是成为了农业科技的奥林匹克村，”Reservoir Farms首席执行官Danny Bernstein表示。“他们承诺赞助入驻项目向创始人传递了一个信号：如果你认真解决实际问题，这里有一个生态系统随时准备支持你——让高潜力团队有机会在真实的种植者条件下训练、快速迭代，并将真正对种植者有用的技术推向市场。”
author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
The Western Growers Center for Innovation & Technology (WGCIT) selected Sami Robotics, a startup developing a multifunctional robotic […]
